color_profiles.name: Classic Rose Quartz
color_profiles.cause: Microscopic pink fibrous inclusion of Ti-bearing Al-phosphate hydroxide mineral distributed through massive quartz; optical scattering.

color_profiles.name: Star Rose Quartz
color_profiles.cause: Rutile needle inclusions in three orientations within quartz produce asterism when cut as cabochon.

visual_notes: Rose quartz has a characteristic soft, diffuse, milky-pink quality from the light-scattering fibrous inclusions. It is never facet-grade transparent in classic form. The color is gentle and warm — not vivid like ruby or pink tourmaline but consistently soft. Star rose quartz cut as cabochon shows the six-rayed star most vividly under a point light source.

optical_effects.fluorescence: Weak blue or orange-pink under UV
optical_effects.asterism: Star rose quartz — six-rayed star from oriented rutile inclusions; best shown in Sri Lankan and Brazilian material
